title: Specify WorkspaceModel (v1)
description: Deterministic, versioned snapshot of project facts produced by `specrun review` per RFC-32 §WorkspaceModel. Per RFC-32 §Persistence and query (v1 decision): `.specify/cache/workspace-model.v1.json` is reserved as the future v2 persistence location; `specrun model query <selector>` is reserved as a future read-only CLI verb. v1 ships `--dump-model` only.

version.description: Per RFC-32 §WorkspaceModel — version discriminant. Breaking indexer output bumps the version; v1 pins the shape defined by this schema.

project_dir.description: Per RFC-32 §WorkspaceModel extraction inputs — scan root (consumer project or framework repo). Typically the project root absolute path; relative `.` is accepted for tests and ephemeral scans.

scan_profile.description: Per RFC-32 §WorkspaceModel extraction inputs — controls which extractors run. `consumer` is the only Phase 2 profile; `framework` is reserved for RFC-34.

artifact_paths.description: Per RFC-32 §WorkspaceModel extraction inputs — optional narrow list. Empty array means the indexer performs a full profile-specific scan per §D1.

languages.description: Per RFC-32 §WorkspaceModel extraction inputs — optional language tokens supplied by caller or inferred from paths.

files.description: Per RFC-32 §Core entity families (v1) — `file` facts produced by the filesystem walk.

frontmatter.description: Per RFC-32 §Core entity families (v1) — `frontmatter` facts from markdown `---` block extraction plus YAML parse.

markdown_sections.description: Per RFC-32 §Core entity families (v1) — `markdown_section` facts from the markdown structure pass.

markdown_links.description: Per RFC-32 §Core entity families (v1) — `markdown_link` facts from the fence-aware link scan.

symlinks.description: Per RFC-32 §Core entity families (v1) — `symlink` facts; recorded but not traversed per §D1.

skills.description: Per RFC-32 §Core entity families (v1) — `skill` facts from `plugins/**/SKILL.md`.

adapter_manifests.description: Per RFC-32 §Core entity families (v1) — `adapter_manifest` facts from `adapters/{sources,targets}/**/adapter.yaml`.

marketplace_entries.description: Per RFC-32 §Core entity families (v1) — `marketplace_entry` facts from `.cursor-plugin/marketplace.json`.

codex_rules.description: Per RFC-32 §Core entity families (v1) — `codex_rule` facts from codex tree discovery (reuses the RFC-28 parser).

text_matches.description: Per RFC-32 §Core entity families (v1) — `text_match` facts from the optional precomputed regex index.

relativePath.description: Per RFC-32 §Stability — paths are project-relative with forward slashes.

sha256Hex.description: Hex-encoded SHA-256 digest (64 lowercase hex characters).

kind.description: Per RFC-32 §D1 — `binary` when the first 8 KiB contains a NUL byte; otherwise `text`.

language.description: Optional language token inferred from extension or supplied by the caller.

sha256.description: Optional content digest; populated when extractors need cross-file identity (e.g. `agent_teams` canonical SHA check).

schema-id.description: Per RFC-32 §Core entity families (v1) — optional schema id the frontmatter declares (matches the registered-schema token shape from §D3).

fields.description: Per RFC-32 §Core entity families (v1) — parsed YAML field map. Per-key shape is rule-specific; the model holds the raw value tree.

level.description: Per RFC-32 §Core entity families (v1) — markdown heading level (1–6).

title.description: Heading text after the leading `#`s, with surrounding whitespace trimmed.

line-start.description: 1-based line of the heading line itself.

line-end.description: 1-based last line that belongs to this section (the line before the next same-or-higher-level heading, or the file's last line).

body-line-count.description: Per RFC-32 §Core entity families (v1) — number of non-heading body lines under the section.

from-path.description: Per RFC-32 §Core entity families (v1) — path of the markdown file containing the link.

to-raw.description: Per RFC-32 §Core entity families (v1) — verbatim link target as authored (relative path, URL, or anchor).

resolves.description: Per RFC-32 §Core entity families (v1) — optional; `true` when the target resolves on disk, `false` for broken/dangling references. Absent for off-tree URLs the indexer did not attempt to resolve.

target.description: Per RFC-32 §Core entity families (v1) — symlink target as recorded by the filesystem (may be relative or absolute).

broken.description: Per RFC-32 §Core entity families (v1) — `true` when the link target does not exist on disk.

name.description: Per RFC-32 §Core entity families (v1) — skill name (matches the `name:` frontmatter field).

plugin.description: Per RFC-32 §Core entity families (v1) — owning plugin slug (the directory under `plugins/`).

frontmatter-ref.description: Per RFC-32 §Core entity families (v1) — path back to the originating `frontmatter` fact so consumers can join through the frontmatter table.

axis.description: Per RFC-32 §Core entity families (v1) — `sources` xor `targets` (the on-disk parent directory under `adapters/`).

version.description: Optional manifest version; absent when the adapter does not pin one.

plugin.description: Per RFC-32 §Core entity families (v1) — plugin slug declared by `.cursor-plugin/marketplace.json`.

path-in-manifest.description: Per RFC-32 §Core entity families (v1) — JSON-pointer-style location inside `marketplace.json` where the entry was discovered.

rule-id.description: Per RFC-32 §Core entity families (v1) — codex rule id (matches the RFC-28 codex-rule schema's `id` regex).

origin.description: Per RFC-32 §Core entity families (v1) — which codex tree contributed the rule (target adapter, source adapter, shared universal tree, or organization-local overlay).

frontmatter-ref.description: Per RFC-32 §Core entity families (v1) — path back to the originating `frontmatter` fact so consumers can join through the frontmatter table.

pattern-id.description: Per RFC-32 §Core entity families (v1) — stable identifier for the precomputed regex pattern that produced this match.
